passages.g: The ant works without supervision while the sluggard sleeps toward poverty.
passages.text: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest. How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

passages.g: Persistent laziness brings poverty as quickly as a armed intruder arrives.
passages.text: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man. A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.

passages.g: Scheming people communicate deception through subtle signals and continuously plot harm.
passages.text: He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers; Frowardness is in his heart, he deviseth mischief continually; he soweth discord.

passages.g: God hates pride, lies, bloodshed, wicked schemes, false testimony, and those who cause discord.
passages.text: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy. These six things doth the LORD hate: yea, seven are an abomination unto him:

passages.g: God abhors arrogance, lies, innocent bloodshed, and the eagerness to harm others.
passages.text: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood, An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief,

passages.g: Keep your parents' teachings bound to your heart at all times.
passages.text: A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren. My son, keep thy father’s commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:

passages.g: Internalize your parents' teachings and let them guide you constantly, asleep and awake.
passages.text: Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck. When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.

passages.g: Instruction is light that guides you along the path of life away from seduction.
passages.text: For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life: To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.

passages.g: Don't lust after seduction; it ruins you as contact with fire burns skin.
passages.text: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids. For by means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread: and the adultress will hunt for the precious life.

passages.g: Adultery inevitably brings harm to everyone involved, like touching fire.
passages.text: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his clothes not be burned? Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned? So he that goeth in to his neighbour’s wife; whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.

passages.g: Stealing from hunger is understandable but adultery destroys your own soul.
passages.text: Men do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry; But if he be found, he shall restore sevenfold; he shall give all the substance of his house.

passages.g: Adultery destroys your soul and brings irreversible shame and dishonor.
passages.text: But whoso committeth adultery with a woman lacketh understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul. A wound and dishonour shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.

passages.g: Jealousy inspires vengeance so fierce that no compensation can satisfy it.
passages.text: For jealousy is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance. He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.

passages.g: Hold onto wisdom's teachings with complete devotion, binding them to your heart.
passages.text: My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee. Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye. Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.

passages.g: Treat wisdom as your closest family to guard yourself against seduction.
passages.text: Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister; and call understanding thy kinswoman: That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.

passages.g: A young man lacking understanding walks unknowingly toward seduction.
passages.text: For at the window of my house I looked through my casement, And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,

passages.g: The young man approaches the seductress's house under cover of darkness.
passages.text: Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house, In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:

passages.g: The seductress is cunning in appearance and heart, unfaithful and constantly seeking prey.
passages.text: And, behold, there met him a woman with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart. (She is loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house: Now is she without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)

passages.g: She seduces him with bold flattery, claiming she's been waiting for him.
passages.text: So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him, I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.

passages.g: She claims she sought him out and has prepared everything for his arrival.
passages.text: Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee. I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved works, with fine linen of Egypt.

passages.g: She promises sensual pleasure and says her husband is away for days.
passages.text: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon. Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves. For the goodman is not at home, he is gone a long journey:

passages.g: She uses flattery and persuasive speech to overcome his resistance.
passages.text: He hath taken a bag of money with him, and will come home at the day appointed. With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.

passages.g: He follows her unknowingly toward destruction, like an ox to slaughter or a bird into a trap.
passages.text: He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks; Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.

passages.g: Don't be drawn toward the seductress's ways and paths.
passages.text: Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth. Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.

passages.g: Seduction has destroyed and killed many; her house leads to death.
passages.text: For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her. Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.

passages.g: Wisdom calls out openly and publicly to everyone.
passages.text: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding put forth her voice? She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths. She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.

passages.g: Wisdom addresses all people, especially the simple and foolish, urging them to gain understanding.
passages.text: Unto you, O men, I call; and my voice is to the sons of man. O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.

passages.g: Wisdom speaks truth and rejects wickedness.
passages.text: Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things. For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.

passages.g: Wisdom's teachings are righteous and clear to those who understand.
passages.text: All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; there is nothing froward or perverse in them. They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.

passages.g: Wisdom is more valuable than wealth and superior to all other pursuits.
passages.text: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold. For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.

passages.g: Wisdom combines practical knowledge with moral integrity, rejecting pride and wickedness.
passages.text: I wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions. The fear of the LORD is to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.

passages.g: Just leadership flows from wisdom.
passages.text: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength. By me kings reign, and princes decree justice. By me princes rule, and nobles, even all the judges of the earth.

passages.g: Those who seek wisdom gain lasting wealth, honor, and righteousness.
passages.text: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me. Riches and honour are with me; yea, durable riches and righteousness.

passages.g: Wisdom's benefits surpass material wealth and guide righteous action.
passages.text: My fruit is better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver. I lead in the way of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of judgment:

passages.g: Wisdom rewards those who pursue it with abundance.
passages.text: That I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasures. The LORD possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.

passages.g: Wisdom is eternal and precedes creation.
passages.text: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was. When there were no depths, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains abounding with water.

passages.g: Wisdom exists before the physical world was formed.
passages.text: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth: While as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the highest part of the dust of the world.

passages.g: Wisdom was present throughout creation.
passages.text: When he prepared the heavens, I was there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth: When he established the clouds above: when he strengthened the fountains of the deep:

passages.g: Wisdom was involved in establishing natural order and creation's foundations.
passages.text: When he gave to the sea his decree, that the waters should not pass his commandment: when he appointed the foundations of the earth:

passages.g: Wisdom is fundamental to creation and delights in humanity.
passages.text: Then I was by him, as one brought up with him: and I was daily his delight, rejoicing always before him; Rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth; and my delights were with the sons of men.

passages.g: Following wisdom brings blessing.
passages.text: Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways. Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.

passages.g: Pursuing wisdom grants life and fulfillment.
passages.text: Blessed is the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors. For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the LORD.

passages.g: Rejecting wisdom harms oneself and invites destruction.
passages.text: But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: all they that hate me love death.

passages.g: Wisdom provides abundant resources and nourishment.
passages.text: Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars: She hath killed her beasts; she hath mingled her wine; she hath also furnished her table.

passages.g: Wisdom's invitation is openly available to everyone.
passages.text: She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city, Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,

passages.g: Accepting wisdom requires abandoning foolishness.
passages.text: Come, eat of my bread, and drink of the wine which I have mingled. Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding.

passages.g: Arrogant people reject correction angrily, but wise people welcome it gratefully.
passages.text: He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked man getteth himself a blot. Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.

passages.g: The wise grow in wisdom through instruction, and moral reverence forms wisdom's foundation.
passages.text: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning. The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.

passages.g: Wisdom extends life, and its rewards or consequences fall on oneself alone.
passages.text: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased. If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but if thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear it.

passages.g: Foolishness speaks loudly but without real knowledge.
passages.text: A foolish woman is clamorous: she is simple, and knoweth nothing. For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city, To call passengers who go right on their ways:

passages.g: Foolishness attracts people through the appeal of forbidden pleasure.
passages.text: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: and as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him, Stolen waters are sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.

passages.g: Paths of foolishness lead to destruction.
passages.text: But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell.

passages.g: Wisdom brings family joy and security, while wickedness yields the opposite.
passages.text: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother. Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.

passages.g: Righteousness and hard work sustain life; wickedness and laziness cause ruin.
passages.text: The LORD will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked. He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.

passages.g: Those who prepare wisely enjoy blessing, while neglect brings shame.
passages.text: He that gathereth in summer is a wise son: but he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame. Blessings are upon the head of the just: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.

passages.g: The righteous are remembered fondly while the wicked fade; humility allows growth but boastfulness invites failure.
passages.text: The memory of the just is blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot. The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.

passages.g: Living with integrity provides security while deception gets exposed and backfires.
passages.text: He that walketh uprightly walketh surely: but he that perverteth his ways shall be known. He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.

passages.g: Righteous speech nourishes life; hatred breeds conflict but love repairs harm.
passages.text: The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked. Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.

passages.g: Understanding produces wisdom while foolishness invites correction and ruin.
passages.text: In the lips of him that hath understanding wisdom is found: but a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding. Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.

passages.g: Wealth provides security while poverty creates vulnerability; righteous work sustains while wickedness perpetuates itself.
passages.text: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city: the destruction of the poor is their poverty. The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.

passages.g: Those who accept correction stay on the path of life; hidden deception through false speech is foolish.
passages.text: He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction: but he that refuseth reproof erreth. He that hideth hatred with lying lips, and he that uttereth a slander, is a fool.

passages.g: Excessive speech invites error while restraint is wisdom; righteous speech has worth while wickedness is empty.
passages.text: In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin: but he that refraineth his lips is wise. The tongue of the just is as choice silver: the heart of the wicked is little worth.

passages.g: Righteous speech nourishes others; foolishness self-destructs; true blessing comes without hidden cost.
passages.text: The lips of the righteous feed many: but fools die for want of wisdom. The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.

passages.g: Fools harm for amusement while the wise use understanding; the wicked reap their own fear while the righteous gain their desires.
passages.text: It is as sport to a fool to do mischief: but a man of understanding hath wisdom. The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.

passages.g: The wicked are temporary while the righteous endure; laziness constantly irritates others.
passages.text: As the whirlwind passeth, so is the wicked no more: but the righteous is an everlasting foundation. As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to them that send him.

passages.g: Moral reverence extends life while wickedness shortens it; the righteous have fulfilling hope.
passages.text: The fear of the LORD prolongeth days: but the years of the wicked shall be shortened. The hope of the righteous shall be gladness: but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.

passages.g: Following righteousness provides strength and permanence; wickedness guarantees ruin.
passages.text: The way of the LORD is strength to the upright: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity. The righteous shall never be removed: but the wicked shall not inhabit the earth.

passages.g: Righteous speech produces wisdom while twisted speech isolates.
passages.text: The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out. The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.

passages.g: Justice pleases while cheating offends; humility brings wisdom while pride invites shame.
passages.text: A false balance is abomination to the LORD: but a just weight is his delight. When pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly is wisdom.

passages.g: Integrity guides and protects while corruption self-destructs; only righteousness survives judgment.
passages.text: The integrity of the upright shall guide them: but the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them. Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death.

passages.g: Righteousness guides toward safety while wickedness causes one's own downfall.
passages.text: The righteousness of the perfect shall direct his way: but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness. The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them: but transgressors shall be taken in their own naughtiness.

passages.g: The wicked die with disappointed hopes while the righteous escape trouble.
passages.text: When a wicked man dieth, his expectation shall perish: and the hope of unjust men perisheth. The righteous is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked cometh in his stead.

passages.g: Hypocrisy harms neighbors while knowledge protects the just; communities flourish through righteous prosperity.
passages.text: An hypocrite with his mouth destroyeth his neighbour: but through knowledge shall the just be delivered. When it goeth well with the righteous, the city rejoiceth: and when the wicked perish, there is shouting.

passages.g: Righteous influence builds communities while wickedness tears them down; wisdom brings restraint and respect.
passages.text: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted: but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked. He that is void of wisdom despiseth his neighbour: but a man of understanding holdeth his peace.

passages.g: Gossip betrays trust while faithfulness protects secrets; collective wisdom prevents ruin.
passages.text: A talebearer revealeth secrets: but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter. Where no counsel is, the people fall: but in the multitude of counsellors there is safety.

passages.g: Guaranteeing for strangers brings harm; graciousness and strength preserve what matters.
passages.text: He that is surety for a stranger shall smart for it: and he that hateth suretiship is sure. A gracious woman retaineth honour: and strong men retain riches.

passages.g: Mercy benefits oneself while cruelty harms oneself; righteousness yields reliable reward.
passages.text: The merciful man doeth good to his own soul: but he that is cruel troubleth his own flesh. The wicked worketh a deceitful work: but to him that soweth righteousness shall be a sure reward.

passages.g: Righteous paths lead to life while evil paths lead to death; twisted hearts displease while upright ones delight.
passages.text: As righteousness tendeth to life: so he that pursueth evil pursueth it to his own death. They that are of a froward heart are abomination to the LORD: but such as are upright in their way are his delight.

passages.g: The wicked face consequences despite unified resistance; the righteous are protected; beauty without discretion is wasted.
passages.text: Though hand join in hand, the wicked shall not be unpunished: but the seed of the righteous shall be delivered. As a jewel of gold in a swine’s snout, so is a fair woman which is without discretion.

passages.g: The righteous seek good and the wicked expect harm; generosity multiplies while hoarding depletes.
passages.text: The desire of the righteous is only good: but the expectation of the wicked is wrath. There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and there is that withholdeth more than is meet, but it tendeth to poverty.

passages.g: Generosity multiplies resources; withholding invites curse while fair dealing brings blessing.
passages.text: The liberal soul shall be made fat: and he that watereth shall be watered also himself. He that withholdeth corn, the people shall curse him: but blessing shall be upon the head of him that selleth it.

passages.g: Seeking good brings favor; seeking harm invites consequences; those depending on wealth fail while the righteous flourish.
passages.text: He that diligently seeketh good procureth favour: but he that seeketh mischief, it shall come unto him. He that trusteth in his riches shall fall; but the righteous shall flourish as a branch.

passages.g: Self-sabotage yields nothing; fools serve the wise; righteousness produces lasting benefit.
passages.text: He that troubleth his own house shall inherit the wind: and the fool shall be servant to the wise of heart. The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life; and he that winneth souls is wise.

passages.g: Both the righteous and wicked face earthly consequences, but the wicked face harsher judgment.
passages.text: Behold, the righteous shall be recompensed in the earth: much more the wicked and the sinner.

passages.g: Embracing correction leads to knowledge; rejecting it shows foolishness.
passages.text: Whoso loveth instruction loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reproof is brutish. A good man obtaineth favour of the LORD: but a man of wicked devices will he condemn.

passages.g: Wickedness cannot build lasting foundations while righteousness endures; virtue strengthens partnership.
passages.text: A man shall not be established by wickedness: but the root of the righteous shall not be moved. A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband: but she that maketh ashamed is as rottenness in his bones.

passages.g: Righteous thinking is sound while wicked counsel deceives; honest speech protects.
passages.text: The thoughts of the righteous are right: but the counsels of the wicked are deceit. The words of the wicked are to lie in wait for blood: but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.

passages.g: The wicked vanish while the righteous endure; wisdom earns esteem while perversion invites scorn.
passages.text: The wicked are overthrown, and are not: but the house of the righteous shall stand. A man shall be commended according to his wisdom: but he that is of a perverse heart shall be despised.

passages.g: Modest provision beats proud poverty; righteousness extends to compassion even toward animals.
passages.text: He that is despised, and hath a servant, is better than he that honoureth himself, and lacketh bread. A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast: but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.

passages.g: Productive work sustains while chasing frivolity shows foolishness; evil perpetuates itself while righteousness bears good fruit.
passages.text: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread: but he that followeth vain persons is void of understanding. The wicked desireth the net of evil men: but the root of the righteous yieldeth fruit.

passages.g: Your words and actions determine whether you're trapped or freed by consequences.
passages.text: The wicked is snared by the transgression of his lips: but the just shall come out of trouble. A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth: and the recompence of a man’s hands shall be rendered unto him.

passages.g: Wisdom is seeing yourself clearly and listening to others; foolishness is self-deception and emotional reactivity.
passages.text: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel is wise. A fool’s wrath is presently known: but a prudent man covereth shame.

passages.g: Speech reveals character—truthfulness shows integrity and nourishes, while deception and harshness harm.
passages.text: He that speaketh truth sheweth forth righteousness: but a false witness deceit. There is that speaketh like the piercings of a sword: but the tongue of the wise is health.

passages.g: Truth endures while lies fail. Evil intention breeds deceit; good intention brings joy.
passages.text: The lip of truth shall be established for ever: but a lying tongue is but for a moment. Deceit is in the heart of them that imagine evil: but to the counsellors of peace is joy.

passages.g: Righteousness protects; wickedness brings suffering.
passages.text: There shall no evil happen to the just: but the wicked shall be filled with mischief. Lying lips are abomination to the LORD: but they that deal truly are his delight.

passages.g: Discretion, diligence, and hard work earn respect; foolishness and laziness bring subjugation.
passages.text: A prudent man concealeth knowledge: but the heart of fools proclaimeth foolishness. The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.

passages.g: Encouragement lifts you; sadness weights you down. Virtue is superior to wickedness's temptation.
passages.text: Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad. The righteous is more excellent than his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them.

passages.g: Diligence creates lasting wealth; laziness leaves you empty.
passages.text: The slothful man roasteth not that which he took in hunting: but the substance of a diligent man is precious. In the way of righteousness is life: and in the pathway thereof there is no death.

passages.g: Listening to correction is wisdom; your actions determine your fate.
passages.text: A wise son heareth his father’s instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke. A man shall eat good by the fruit of his mouth: but the soul of the transgressors shall eat violence.

passages.g: Restraint in speech and diligence protect and prosper you.
passages.text: He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction. The soul of the sluggard desireth, and hath nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.

passages.g: Righteousness protects and commands respect; wickedness brings shame and ruin.
passages.text: A righteous man hateth lying: but a wicked man is loathsome, and cometh to shame. Righteousness keepeth him that is upright in the way: but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.

passages.g: True wealth isn't appearance; financial security affects how you're treated.
passages.text: There is that maketh himself rich, yet hath nothing: there is that maketh himself poor, yet hath great riches. The ransom of a man’s life are his riches: but the poor heareth not rebuke.

passages.g: Virtue endures while vice fails. Humility and counsel prevent conflict.
passages.text: The light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out. Only by pride cometh contention: but with the well advised is wisdom.

passages.g: Honest work builds wealth; stolen gains disappear. Fulfilled hopes revive you; delayed hopes demoralize you.
passages.text: Wealth gotten by vanity shall be diminished: but he that gathereth by labour shall increase. Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but when the desire cometh, it is a tree of life.

passages.g: Following good instruction saves you from ruin.
passages.text: Whoso despiseth the word shall be destroyed: but he that feareth the commandment shall be rewarded. The law of the wise is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.

passages.g: Good judgment wins favor; foolishness creates hardship.
passages.text: Good understanding giveth favour: but the way of transgressors is hard. Every prudent man dealeth with knowledge: but a fool layeth open his folly.

passages.g: Faithfulness and receptiveness to correction earn success and respect.
passages.text: A wicked messenger falleth into mischief: but a faithful ambassador is health. Poverty and shame shall be to him that refuseth instruction: but he that regardeth reproof shall be honoured.

passages.g: Achieving your goals satisfies you; your companions shape who you become.
passages.text: The desire accomplished is sweet to the soul: but it is abomination to fools to depart from evil. He that walketh with wise men shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed.

passages.g: Wrongdoing brings pursuing consequences; virtue creates lasting benefit for descendants.
passages.text: Evil pursueth sinners: but to the righteous good shall be repayed. A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children’s children: and the wealth of the sinner is laid up for the just.

passages.g: Hard work produces abundance; discipline shows love.
passages.text: Much food is in the tillage of the poor: but there is that is destroyed for want of judgment. He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.

passages.g: Virtue brings satisfaction; wickedness brings want.
passages.text: The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul: but the belly of the wicked shall want.

passages.g: Wisdom builds; foolishness destroys.
passages.text: Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands. He that walketh in his uprightness feareth the LORD: but he that is perverse in his ways despiseth him.

passages.g: Arrogance fails; diligent work yields returns.
passages.text: In the mouth of the foolish is a rod of pride: but the lips of the wise shall preserve them. Where no oxen are, the crib is clean: but much increase is by the strength of the ox.

passages.g: Truthful witnesses tell truth. Scornful people can't learn; understanding people grasp easily.
passages.text: A faithful witness will not lie: but a false witness will utter lies. A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth.

passages.g: Avoid foolish company. Self-awareness is wise; self-deception is foolish.
passages.text: Go from the presence of a foolish man, when thou perceivest not in him the lips of knowledge. The wisdom of the prudent is to understand his way: but the folly of fools is deceit.

passages.g: Fools mock wrong; righteous are respected. Only you truly know your own pain and joy.
passages.text: Fools make a mock at sin: but among the righteous there is favour. The heart knoweth his own bitterness; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy.

passages.g: Wickedness crumbles; virtue stands firm. What looks right can lead to ruin.
passages.text: The house of the wicked shall be overthrown: but the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish. There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death.

passages.g: Happiness can mask sadness within. Character determines your lasting satisfaction.
passages.text: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth is heaviness. The backslider in heart shall be filled with his own ways: and a good man shall be satisfied from himself.

passages.g: Wisdom is discernment and caution; folly is credulity and overconfidence.
passages.text: The simple believeth every word: but the prudent man looketh well to his going. A wise man feareth, and departeth from evil: but the fool rageth, and is confident.

passages.g: Quick anger is foolish; slow anger shows wisdom.
passages.text: He that is soon angry dealeth foolishly: and a man of wicked devices is hated. The simple inherit folly: but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

passages.g: Goodness commands respect; wealth determines how you're treated.
passages.text: The evil bow before the good; and the wicked at the gates of the righteous. The poor is hated even of his own neighbour: but the rich hath many friends.

passages.g: Compassion brings happiness; good planning is rewarded.
passages.text: He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy is he. Do they not err that devise evil? but mercy and truth shall be to them that devise good.

passages.g: Work produces wealth; talk produces poverty.
passages.text: In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury. The crown of the wise is their riches: but the foolishness of fools is folly.

passages.g: Truthful testimony redeems; principled living protects your family.
passages.text: A true witness delivereth souls: but a deceitful witness speaketh lies. In the fear of the LORD is strong confidence: and his children shall have a place of refuge.

passages.g: Principled living sustains life; leaders depend on followers.
passages.text: The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death. In the multitude of people is the king’s honour: but in the want of people is the destruction of the prince.

passages.g: Emotional control and contentment sustain you; anger and envy destroy you.
passages.text: He that is slow to wrath is of great understanding: but he that is hasty of spirit exalteth folly. A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones.

passages.g: How you treat the vulnerable matters; virtue brings peace.
passages.text: He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker: but he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor. The wicked is driven away in his wickedness: but the righteous hath hope in his death.

passages.g: Wisdom is hidden while folly is obvious; national virtue determines national strength.
passages.text: Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but that which is in the midst of fools is made known. Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin is a reproach to any people.

passages.g: Leaders reward competence and punish failure.
passages.text: The king’s favour is toward a wise servant: but his wrath is against him that causeth shame.

passages.g: Gentleness prevents conflict; harsh words escalate it.
passages.text: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger. The tongue of the wise useth knowledge aright: but the mouth of fools poureth out foolishness.

passages.g: Nothing escapes accountability. Healthy speech nourishes; twisted speech damages.
passages.text: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, beholding the evil and the good. A wholesome tongue is a tree of life: but perverseness therein is a breach in the spirit.

passages.g: Accepting correction is wisdom; virtue creates stability.
passages.text: A fool despiseth his father’s instruction: but he that regardeth reproof is prudent. In the house of the righteous is much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble.

passages.g: Wise people share knowledge. Character matters more than action.
passages.text: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish doeth not so. The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD: but the prayer of the upright is his delight.

passages.g: Virtue is loved; wickedness is rejected. Rejecting correction seals your fate.
passages.text: The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the LORD: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness. Correction is grievous unto him that forsaketh the way: and he that hateth reproof shall die.

passages.g: Everything is accountable. Pride blocks learning.
passages.text: Hell and destruction are before the LORD: how much more then the hearts of the children of men? A scorner loveth not one that reproveth him: neither will he go unto the wise.

passages.g: Inner joy shows outward and affects well-being. Wise people learn; fools feed ignorance.
passages.text: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance: but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken. The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness.

passages.g: Your emotional state transforms experience. Integrity with little beats wealth with trouble.
passages.text: All the days of the afflicted are evil: but he that is of a merry heart hath a continual feast. Better is little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith.

passages.g: Relationships matter more than comfort. Emotional control determines whether you escalate or resolve conflict.
passages.text: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is, than a stalled ox and hatred therewith. A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but he that is slow to anger appeaseth strife.

passages.g: Laziness creates obstacles; wisdom clears your path.
passages.text: The way of the slothful man is as an hedge of thorns: but the way of the righteous is made plain. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.

passages.g: Wisdom requires integrity. Good planning needs multiple perspectives.
passages.text: Folly is joy to him that is destitute of wisdom: but a man of understanding walketh uprightly. Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.

passages.g: Good conversation satisfies you. Timely words are precious.
passages.text: A man hath joy by the answer of his mouth: and a word spoken in due season, how good is it! The way of life is above to the wise, that he may depart from hell beneath.

passages.g: Pride causes ruin; integrity is stable.
passages.text: The LORD will destroy the house of the proud: but he will establish the border of the widow. The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the LORD: but the words of the pure are pleasant words.

passages.g: Greed damages your household; integrity protects it.
passages.text: He that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house; but he that hateth gifts shall live. The heart of the righteous studieth to answer: but the mouth of the wicked poureth out evil things.

passages.g: Virtue connects you; vice isolates you.
passages.text: The LORD is far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous. The light of the eyes rejoiceth the heart: and a good report maketh the bones fat.

passages.g: Accepting correction makes you wise; rejecting it harms you.
passages.text: The ear that heareth the reproof of life abideth among the wise. He that refuseth instruction despiseth his own soul: but he that heareth reproof getteth understanding.

passages.g: Respect for principles teaches wisdom; humility precedes honor.
passages.text: The fear of the LORD is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.

passages.g: People justify themselves, but actual character is revealed.
passages.text: The preparations of the heart in man, and the answer of the tongue, is from the LORD. All the ways of a man are clean in his own eyes; but the LORD weigheth the spirits.

passages.g: Committing yourself to principles stabilizes your work.
passages.text: Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established. The LORD hath made all things for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.

passages.g: Pride is always punished. Mercy and truth overcome wrongdoing.
passages.text: Every one that is proud in heart is an abomination to the LORD: though hand join in hand, he shall not be unpunished. By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD men depart from evil.

passages.g: Virtue attracts peace even from enemies. Integrity beats wealth without it.
passages.text: When a man’s ways please the LORD, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him. Better is a little with righteousness than great revenues without right.

passages.g: Humans make plans but ultimate outcomes are determined by forces beyond their control.
passages.text: A man’s heart deviseth his way: but the LORD directeth his steps. A divine sentence is in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in judgment.

passages.g: Fairness is fundamental; rulers who act wickedly undermine their own authority.
passages.text: A just weight and balance are the LORD’s: all the weights of the bag are his work. It is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness: for the throne is established by righteousness.

passages.g: Honest speech pleases those in authority, and wisdom can defuse anger.
passages.text: Righteous lips are the delight of kings; and they love him that speaketh right. The wrath of a king is as messengers of death: but a wise man will pacify it.

passages.g: Wisdom and understanding are more valuable than wealth.
passages.text: In the light of the king’s countenance is life; and his favour is as a cloud of the latter rain. How much better is it to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver!

passages.g: Living virtuously protects you from harm, while pride causes downfall.
passages.text: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul. Pride goeth before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.

passages.g: Humility is preferable to arrogance, and wise judgment brings positive outcomes.
passages.text: Better it is to be of an humble spirit with the lowly, than to divide the spoil with the proud. He that handleth a matter wisely shall find good: and whoso trusteth in the LORD, happy is he.

passages.g: Wisdom makes people prudent, kind speech enhances learning, and genuine understanding nourishes life.
passages.text: The wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning. Understanding is a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools is folly.

passages.g: Wisdom shapes how you speak and what you learn, and kind words have a healing effect.
passages.text: The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips. Pleasant words are as an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

passages.g: What appears right can actually lead to ruin, and people naturally work to satisfy their own needs.
passages.text: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof are the ways of death. He that laboureth laboureth for himself; for his mouth craveth it of him.

passages.g: Malicious people spread harm through their words and actions, sowing discord among friends.
passages.text: An ungodly man diggeth up evil: and in his lips there is as a burning fire. A froward man soweth strife: and a whisperer separateth chief friends.

passages.g: Violent people deliberately corrupt others and bring harm through their scheming.
passages.text: A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way that is not good. He shutteth his eyes to devise froward things: moving his lips he bringeth evil to pass.

passages.g: Age brings honor when lived righteously, and self-control is more powerful than military strength.
passages.text: The hoary head is a crown of glory, if it be found in the way of righteousness. He that is slow to anger is better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.

passages.g: Outcomes are ultimately determined by forces beyond human control.
passages.text: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the LORD.

passages.g: Peace and simplicity are better than abundance with conflict, and wisdom is more valuable than status.
passages.text: Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife. A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.

passages.g: Character is tested like precious metals, and wickedness finds agreement with falsehood.
passages.text: The fining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold: but the LORD trieth the hearts. A wicked doer giveth heed to false lips; and a liar giveth ear to a naughty tongue.

passages.g: Mocking the poor is an offense, delighting in others' suffering brings punishment, and family ties bind generations.
passages.text: Whoso mocketh the poor reproacheth his Maker: and he that is glad at calamities shall not be unpunished. Children’s children are the crown of old men; and the glory of children are their fathers.

passages.g: Eloquence is wasted on fools and dishonesty is especially damaging in leaders, while generous gifts create opportunity.
passages.text: Excellent speech becometh not a fool: much less do lying lips a prince. A gift is as a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoever it turneth, it prospereth.

passages.g: Overlooking wrongs strengthens relationships while bringing up the past damages them, and wise people are receptive to criticism.
passages.text: He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends. A reproof entereth more into a wise man than an hundred stripes into a fool.

passages.g: Evil brings harsh consequences, and dealing with a foolish person is as dangerous as facing a threatened animal.
passages.text: An evil man seeketh only rebellion: therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him. Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.

passages.g: Repaying good with evil creates lasting harm, and conflict should be stopped before it escalates.
passages.text: Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house. The beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water: therefore leave off contention, before it be meddled with.

passages.g: Defending the guilty and condemning the innocent are equally corrupt, and fools cannot buy wisdom because they don't genuinely desire it.
passages.text: He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both are abomination to the LORD. Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?

passages.g: True friendship is constant through all circumstances, while foolish people make reckless commitments.
passages.text: A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity. A man void of understanding striketh hands, and becometh surety in the presence of his friend.

passages.g: Those who pursue conflict and boast about themselves invite their own destruction.
passages.text: He loveth transgression that loveth strife: and he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction. He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.

passages.g: Raising a foolish child brings sorrow, while joy has a healing effect and despair causes deterioration.
passages.text: He that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow: and the father of a fool hath no joy. A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.

passages.g: Wickedness uses bribery to corrupt justice, while the wise are discerning and the foolish are unfocused.
passages.text: A wicked man taketh a gift out of the bosom to pervert the ways of judgment. Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.

passages.g: Foolish children cause their parents grief, and punishing the innocent is an injustice.
passages.text: A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitterness to her that bare him. Also to punish the just is not good, nor to strike princes for equity.

passages.g: Knowledge is reflected in measured speech, and even fools can seem wise by staying quiet.
passages.text: He that hath knowledge spareth his words: and a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit. Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: and he that shutteth his lips is esteemed a man of understanding.

passages.g: Ambitious seekers isolate themselves to pursue wisdom, but fools don't genuinely value understanding.
passages.text: Through desire a man, having separated himself, seeketh and intermeddleth with all wisdom. A fool hath no delight in understanding, but that his heart may discover itself.

passages.g: Wickedness brings contempt, while wise speech is profound and flows naturally.
passages.text: When the wicked cometh, then cometh also contempt, and with ignominy reproach. The words of a man’s mouth are as deep waters, and the wellspring of wisdom as a flowing brook.

passages.g: Unjust judgment that favors the wicked is wrong, and fools invite punishment through their reckless speech.
passages.text: It is not good to accept the person of the wicked, to overthrow the righteous in judgment. A fool’s lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for strokes.

passages.g: Fools bring about their own downfall through careless speech, and gossip causes lasting injury.
passages.text: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are the snare of his soul. The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

passages.g: Laziness is as destructive as waste, and virtue provides security.
passages.text: He also that is slothful in his work is brother to him that is a great waster. The name of the LORD is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.

passages.g: Wealth creates a false sense of security, pride leads to destruction, and humility precedes recognition.
passages.text: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, and as an high wall in his own conceit. Before destruction the heart of man is haughty, and before honour is humility.

passages.g: Responding without listening is foolish, and while the spirit can sustain hardships, psychological damage is difficult to overcome.
passages.text: He that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly and shame unto him. The spirit of a man will sustain his infirmity; but a wounded spirit who can bear?

passages.g: The prudent and wise actively seek knowledge, and generosity opens pathways to influence.
passages.text: The heart of the prudent getteth knowledge; and the ear of the wise seeketh knowledge. A man’s gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men.

passages.g: Everyone appears just in their own case until proven otherwise, and impartial judgment settles disputes.
passages.text: He that is first in his own cause seemeth just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him. The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth between the mighty.

passages.g: Repairing a damaged relationship with family is harder than conquering a fortified city.
passages.text: A brother offended is harder to be won than a strong city: and their contentions are like the bars of a castle.

passages.g: You experience the consequences of your own speech, and words have the power to determine your fate.
passages.text: A man’s belly shall be satisfied with the fruit of his mouth; and with the increase of his lips shall he be filled. Death and life are in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.

passages.g: Finding a good partner is blessed, while poverty teaches politeness and wealth breeds arrogance.
passages.text: Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the LORD. The poor useth intreaties; but the rich answereth roughly.

passages.g: Cultivating friendship requires showing friendliness, and some friendships run deeper than family bonds.
passages.text: A man that hath friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.

passages.g: Integrity is more valuable than wealth, and both ignorance and haste lead to harm.
passages.text: Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity, than he that is perverse in his lips, and is a fool. Also, that the soul be without knowledge, it is not good; and he that hasteth with his feet sinneth.

passages.g: Foolishness and resentment cause self-destruction, while wealth attracts friends and poverty causes isolation.
passages.text: The foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the LORD. Wealth maketh many friends; but the poor is separated from his neighbour.

passages.g: Dishonesty brings punishment, and people are drawn to those with power and generosity.
passages.text: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall not escape. Many will intreat the favour of the prince: and every man is a friend to him that giveth gifts.

passages.g: Poverty leads to rejection even by family, and the poor are abandoned despite their pleas.
passages.text: All the brethren of the poor do hate him: how much more do his friends go far from him? he pursueth them with words, yet they are wanting to him.

passages.g: Pursuing wisdom is an act of self-love, and dishonesty leads to ruin.
passages.text: He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good. A false witness shall not be unpunished, and he that speaketh lies shall perish.

passages.g: Low-status people should not be arrogant, and self-control and forgiveness are marks of character.
passages.text: Delight is not seemly for a fool; much less for a servant to have rule over princes. The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.

passages.g: A ruler's anger is devastating while their favor is refreshing, foolish children bring calamity, and constant spousal conflict is exhausting.
passages.text: The king’s wrath is as the roaring of a lion; but his favour is as dew upon the grass. A foolish son is the calamity of his father: and the contentions of a wife are a continual dropping.

passages.g: Wealth is inherited but a wise partner is a blessing, and idleness leads to deprivation.
passages.text: House and riches are the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife is from the LORD. Slothfulness casteth into a deep sleep; and an idle soul shall suffer hunger.

passages.g: Following principles preserves your life while rejecting them causes ruin, and helping the poor ultimately helps yourself.
passages.text: He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die. He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.

passages.g: Children should be disciplined early, and those prone to anger will face repeated punishment.
passages.text: Chasten thy son while there is hope, and let not thy soul spare for his crying. A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment: for if thou deliver him, yet thou must do it again.

passages.g: Accepting counsel leads to wisdom, and while people have many plans, the right guidance ultimately prevails.
passages.text: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end. There are many devices in a man’s heart; nevertheless the counsel of the LORD, that shall stand.

passages.g: People's natural desire is for kindness, honesty is more valuable than wealth, and faith in principles brings peace and security.
passages.text: The desire of a man is his kindness: and a poor man is better than a liar. The fear of the LORD tendeth to life: and he that hath it shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.

passages.g: Laziness prevents even basic effort, and while consequences teach the foolish, wise people learn from explanation.
passages.text: A slothful man hideth his hand in his bosom, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again. Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware: and reprove one that hath understanding, and he will understand knowledge.

passages.g: Mistreating parents brings disgrace, and you should reject teachings that contradict wisdom.
passages.text: He that wasteth his father, and chaseth away his mother, is a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach. Cease, my son, to hear the instruction that causeth to err from the words of knowledge.

passages.g: Wicked witnesses mock justice and consume wrongdoing, and fools and mockers face inevitable punishment.
passages.text: An ungodly witness scorneth judgment: and the mouth of the wicked devoureth iniquity. Judgments are prepared for scorners, and stripes for the back of fools.

passages.g: Alcohol deceives and leads people astray, and provoking those in power ultimately harms yourself.
passages.text: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise. The fear of a king is as the roaring of a lion: whoso provoketh him to anger sinneth against his own soul.

passages.g: Avoiding conflict is admirable while fools court trouble, and idleness leads to deprivation.
passages.text: It is an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling. The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; therefore shall he beg in harvest, and have nothing.

passages.g: People's true intentions are hidden but the wise can discern them, and while everyone claims goodness, truly faithful people are rare.
passages.text: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out. Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness: but a faithful man who can find?

passages.g: Living with integrity benefits your descendants, and just rulers use their authority to eliminate wrongdoing.
passages.text: The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him. A king that sitteth in the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.

passages.g: No one can claim to be free from wrongdoing, and dishonesty in any form of judgment is contemptible.
passages.text: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin? Divers weights, and divers measures, both of them are alike abomination to the LORD.

passages.g: Your character is revealed by your actions.
passages.text: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work be pure, and whether it be right. The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the LORD hath made even both of them.

passages.g: Laziness causes poverty while diligence ensures provision; negotiators understate value then boast their deal.
passages.text: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, and thou shalt be satisfied with bread. It is naught, it is naught, saith the buyer: but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.

passages.g: Knowledge is more valuable than precious materials; require collateral from those making risky guarantees.
passages.text: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel. Take his garment that is surety for a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

passages.g: Dishonest gain brings initial pleasure but ultimate regret; sound decisions require good counsel.
passages.text: Bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel. Every purpose is established by counsel: and with good advice make war.

passages.g: Avoid gossipers and flatterers; disrespecting parents leads to ruin.
passages.text: He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips. Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.

passages.g: Wealth gained quickly doesn't end well; seek justice through divine means, not personal revenge.
passages.text: An inheritance may be gotten hastily at the beginning; but the end thereof shall not be blessed. Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the LORD, and he shall save thee.

passages.g: Dishonest business practices are wrong; people cannot understand their own destiny.
passages.text: Divers weights are an abomination unto the LORD; and a false balance is not good. Man’s goings are of the LORD; how can a man then understand his own way?

passages.g: Acting on important matters without understanding consequences is dangerous; wise leaders eliminate wickedness.
passages.text: It is a snare to the man who devoureth that which is holy, and after vows to make enquiry. A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.

passages.g: Conscience examines inner character; mercy and truth sustain a leader's authority.
passages.text: The spirit of man is the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts of the belly. Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy.

passages.g: Youth's strength and age's wisdom are both valuable; hardship can correct inner character.
passages.text: The glory of young men is their strength: and the beauty of old men is the grey head. The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil: so do stripes the inward parts of the belly.

passages.g: Divine will ultimately directs outcomes; people rationalize their actions while God judges their true hearts.
passages.text: The king’s heart is in the hand of the LORD, as the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will. Every way of a man is right in his own eyes: but the LORD pondereth the hearts.

passages.g: Justice matters more than religious ritual; pride and arrogance are fundamental failures.
passages.text: To do justice and judgment is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice. An high look, and a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked, is sin.

passages.g: Careful planning brings prosperity while hasty thinking brings scarcity; dishonest wealth is unstable.
passages.text: The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; but of every one that is hasty only to want. The getting of treasures by a lying tongue is a vanity tossed to and fro of them that seek death.

passages.g: The wicked destroy themselves through injustice; the morally pure act rightly.
passages.text: The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment. The way of man is froward and strange: but as for the pure, his work is right.

passages.g: Solitude is preferable to constant conflict; the wicked harm those around them.
passages.text: It is better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house. The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.

passages.g: Observing consequences and receiving instruction both create wisdom.
passages.text: When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.

passages.g: God destroys the wicked; ignoring others' suffering brings the same upon yourself.
passages.text: The righteous man wisely considereth the house of the wicked: but God overthroweth the wicked for their wickedness. Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.

passages.g: Discreet gifts calm anger; justice rewards the righteous and destroys the wicked.
passages.text: A gift in secret pacifieth anger: and a reward in the bosom strong wrath. It is joy to the just to do judgment: but destruction shall be to the workers of iniquity.

passages.g: Abandoning wisdom leads to ruin; pursuit of pleasure leads to poverty.
passages.text: The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding shall remain in the congregation of the dead. He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.

passages.g: The wicked suffer while the righteous are spared; solitude beats living with conflict.
passages.text: The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright. It is better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.

passages.g: The wise accumulate wealth while fools squander it; pursuing righteousness brings life and honor.
passages.text: There is treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up. He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.

passages.g: Wisdom overcomes military strength; controlling speech prevents trouble.
passages.text: A wise man scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof. Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.

passages.g: Pride breeds wrath; laziness leads to ruin; the righteous give generously.
passages.text: Proud and haughty scorner is his name, who dealeth in proud wrath. The desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour. He coveteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giveth and spareth not.

passages.g: Wicked offerings with wicked intent are worthless; false witnesses perish while honest speakers endure.
passages.text: The sacrifice of the wicked is abomination: how much more, when he bringeth it with a wicked mind? A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.

passages.g: The wicked lack direction while the upright are guided; divine will surpasses all human wisdom; safety comes from God.
passages.text: A wicked man hardeneth his face: but as for the upright, he directeth his way. There is no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the LORD. The horse is prepared against the day of battle: but safety is of the LORD.

passages.g: A good reputation is worth more than wealth; rich and poor are equal before God.
passages.text: A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favour rather than silver and gold. The rich and poor meet together: the LORD is the maker of them all.

passages.g: The wise foresee danger and avoid it while fools suffer; humility and reverence bring prosperity.
passages.text: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished. By humility and the fear of the LORD are riches, and honour, and life.

passages.g: The wicked face inherent dangers; careful avoidance protects you; childhood training shapes lifelong character.
passages.text: Thorns and snares are in the way of the froward: he that doth keep his soul shall be far from them. Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.

passages.g: The rich dominate the poor through debt; wrongdoing produces emptiness and anger leads to failure.
passages.text: The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender. He that soweth iniquity shall reap vanity: and the rod of his anger shall fail.

passages.g: Generosity to the poor brings blessing; removing mockers eliminates conflict.
passages.text: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed; for he giveth of his bread to the poor. Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease.

passages.g: Purity of heart and gracious speech win favor from leaders; God protects truth and destroys falsehood.
passages.text: He that loveth pureness of heart, for the grace of his lips the king shall be his friend. The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, and he overthroweth the words of the transgressor.

passages.g: The lazy make excuses to avoid effort; seductive people trap those God rejects.
passages.text: The slothful man saith, There is a lion without, I shall be slain in the streets. The mouth of strange women is a deep pit: he that is abhorred of the LORD shall fall therein.

passages.g: Children require correction to overcome natural foolishness; oppressing the poor or flattering the rich leads to want.
passages.text: Foolishness is bound in the heart of a child; but the rod of correction shall drive it far from him. He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.

passages.g: Internalizing wisdom allows you to speak it naturally.
passages.text: Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge. For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.

passages.g: Wisdom teaches you to trust in God and answer truthfully.
passages.text: That thy trust may be in the LORD, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee. Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,

passages.g: Don't exploit the poor or oppress the vulnerable.
passages.text: That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee? Rob not the poor, because he is poor: neither oppress the afflicted in the gate:

passages.g: God vindicates the oppressed; avoid angry people lest you adopt their destructive ways.
passages.text: For the LORD will plead their cause, and spoil the soul of those that spoiled them. Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go: Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.

passages.g: Avoid making risky financial pledges for others; respect established property boundaries.
passages.text: Be not thou one of them that strike hands, or of them that are sureties for debts. If thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee? Remove not the ancient landmark, which thy fathers have set.

passages.g: Diligence in work leads to prominence and influence.
passages.text: Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before mean men.

passages.g: Show restraint when dining with powerful people; don't overindulge.
passages.text: When thou sittest to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what is before thee: And put a knife to thy throat, if thou be a man given to appetite. Be not desirous of his dainties: for they are deceitful meat.

passages.g: Don't obsess over accumulating wealth; riches are unreliable.
passages.text: Labour not to be rich: cease from thine own wisdom. Wilt thou set thine eyes upon that which is not? for riches certainly make themselves wings; they fly away as an eagle toward heaven.

passages.g: Avoid gifts from those with selfish motives; their hospitality conceals true intent.
passages.text: Eat thou not the bread of him that hath an evil eye, neither desire thou his dainty meats: For as he thinketh in his heart, so is he: Eat and drink, saith he to thee; but his heart is not with thee.

passages.g: Gifts from the insincere lead to regret; don't waste wisdom on fools.
passages.text: The morsel which thou hast eaten shalt thou vomit up, and lose thy sweet words. Speak not in the ears of a fool: for he will despise the wisdom of thy words.

passages.g: Respect property boundaries and don't exploit the vulnerable.
passages.text: Remove not the old landmark; and enter not into the fields of the fatherless: For their redeemer is mighty; he shall plead their cause with thee.

passages.g: Accept instruction; discipline children for their benefit.
passages.text: Apply thine heart unto instruction, and thine ears to the words of knowledge. Withhold not correction from the child: for if thou beatest him with the rod, he shall not die.

passages.g: Disciplining children saves them from ruin; wise children bring parents joy.
passages.text: Thou shalt beat him with the rod, and shalt deliver his soul from hell. My son, if thine heart be wise, my heart shall rejoice, even mine. Yea, my reins shall rejoice, when thy lips speak right things.

passages.g: Don't envy the wicked; reverence for God preserves your hope.
passages.text: Let not thine heart envy sinners: but be thou in the fear of the LORD all the day long. For surely there is an end; and thine expectation shall not be cut off.

passages.g: Listen to wisdom and avoid intemperate companions.
passages.text: Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way. Be not among winebibbers; among riotous eaters of flesh:

passages.g: Indulgence leads to poverty; honor your aging parents.
passages.text: For the drunkard and the glutton shall come to poverty: and drowsiness shall clothe a man with rags. Hearken unto thy father that begat thee, and despise not thy mother when she is old.

passages.g: Acquire and treasure wisdom; raising wise children brings deep joy.
passages.text: Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding. The father of the righteous shall greatly rejoice: and he that begetteth a wise child shall have joy of him.

passages.g: Your success brings joy to your parents; avoid seductive people.
passages.text: Thy father and thy mother shall be glad, and she that bare thee shall rejoice. My son, give me thine heart, and let thine eyes observe my ways. For a whore is a deep ditch; and a strange woman is a narrow pit.

passages.g: Seductive people trap others and multiply suffering.
passages.text: She also lieth in wait as for a prey, and increaseth the transgressors among men. Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath babbling? who hath wounds without cause? who hath redness of eyes?

passages.g: Avoid dwelling on the pleasures of alcohol.
passages.text: They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to seek mixed wine. Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, when it moveth itself aright.

passages.g: Alcohol harms you secretly while impairing your judgment about seductive people.
passages.text: At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder. Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thine heart shall utter perverse things.

passages.g: Intoxication leaves you helpless and disoriented.
passages.text: Yea, thou shalt be as he that lieth down in the midst of the sea, or as he that lieth upon the top of a mast.

passages.g: The addicted person is numb to harm and craves more despite consequences.
passages.text: They have stricken me, shalt thou say, and I was not sick; they have beaten me, and I felt it not: when shall I awake? I will seek it yet again.

passages.g: Don't envy the wicked who plan destruction; wisdom builds and sustains.
passages.text: Be not thou envious against evil men, neither desire to be with them. For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief. Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established:

passages.g: Knowledge brings both material wealth and personal strength.
passages.text: And by knowledge shall the chambers be filled with all precious and pleasant riches. A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.

passages.g: Success in conflict requires wise counsel; fools lack the wisdom for leadership.
passages.text: For by wise counsel thou shalt make thy war: and in multitude of counsellors there is safety. Wisdom is too high for a fool: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.

passages.g: Planning evil reveals your nature; foolish thinking is sinful; weakness in hardship shows limited strength.
passages.text: He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a mischievous person. The thought of foolishness is sin: and the scorner is an abomination to men. If thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength is small.

passages.g: Failing to rescue those in mortal danger is wrong.
passages.text: If thou forbear to deliver them that are drawn unto death, and those that are ready to be slain;

passages.g: You cannot escape divine judgment by claiming ignorance, as God knows all your deeds.
passages.text: If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider it? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth not he know it? and shall not he render to every man according to his works?

passages.g: It is good to enjoy pleasant things.
passages.text: My son, eat thou honey, because it is good; and the honeycomb, which is sweet to thy taste:

passages.g: Pursuing wisdom brings reward and fulfillment.
passages.text: So shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.

passages.g: The righteous persevere through repeated falls while the wicked ultimately face ruin.
passages.text: Lay not wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; spoil not his resting place: For a just man falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.

passages.g: Do not gloat when your enemy falls.
passages.text: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth: Lest the LORD see it, and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

passages.g: Do not envy the wicked, as they will ultimately be destroyed.
passages.text: Fret not thyself because of evil men, neither be thou envious at the wicked: For there shall be no reward to the evil man; the candle of the wicked shall be put out.

passages.g: Avoid involvement with those who rebel against authority, as their downfall comes suddenly.
passages.text: My son, fear thou the LORD and the king: and meddle not with them that are given to change: For their calamity shall rise suddenly; and who knoweth the ruin of them both?

passages.g: Justice requires impartiality; declaring the wicked righteous brings universal condemnation.
passages.text: These things also belong to the wise. It is not good to have respect of persons in judgment. He that saith unto the wicked, Thou are righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:

passages.g: Those who give honest rebuke or speak rightly earn respect and favor.
passages.text: But to them that rebuke him shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come upon them. Every man shall kiss his lips that giveth a right answer.

passages.g: Establish your livelihood first before building other security, and avoid lying and false witness.
passages.text: Prepare thy work without, and make it fit for thyself in the field; and afterwards build thine house. Be not a witness against thy neighbour without cause; and deceive not with thy lips.

passages.g: Do not seek retaliation for wrongs done to you.
passages.text: Say not, I will do so to him as he hath done to me: I will render to the man according to his work. I went by the field of the slothful, and by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;
